title: hymenophyllum cuneatum
intro: Hymenophyllum cuneatum, also known as the wedged scale fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, characterized by its unique, wedge-shaped fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Hymenophyllum cuneatum has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and acting as an antiseptic. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
